title: Feature registry frontmatter
$comment: Additive history (WU 2.14, F2.4): Phase 1 baseline schema — correlation_id, state, involved_repos, autonomy_default, task_graph (tasks: id, type, depends_on, assigned_repo, autonomy). Phase 2 additions: optional required_templates array on task objects (WU 2.6) — generator template tokens the task needs from its assigned_repo; populated by the human during drafting or plan_review, never by the task-decomposition skill at v1.
description: YAML frontmatter on a /features/<id>.md file. Fields are the machine-readable contract for feature state; prose in the body is for humans. See orchestrator-architecture.md §3 and §6.1.

correlation_id.description: Feature-level correlation ID. Task-level IDs are not valid here.

state.description: Current feature state from the feature state machine in §6.1.

involved_repos.description: Component repositories touched by this feature, as `owner/repo` strings.

autonomy_default.description: Feature-level autonomy default; may be overridden per task during plan review.

task_graph.description: Approved task graph. Minimal v0 shape: each task has an ID, type, dependency list, and assigned repo. Expands as planning matures.

id.description: Task ID local to the feature (TNN). Combined with the feature correlation ID, this forms the task-level correlation ID.

depends_on.description: Task IDs within this feature that must reach `done` before this task becomes `ready`.

assigned_repo.description: `owner/repo` that will host this task's GitHub issue and (for implementation tasks) the resulting PR.

autonomy.description: Per-task autonomy override. If absent, the task inherits the feature-level `autonomy_default`. Set by the PM agent during task decomposition when safety policy requires a stricter level than the feature default (e.g. `qa_execution` is never `auto`), or by the human during plan review.

required_templates.description: Generator template tokens this task requires from its `assigned_repo`. Consumed by the PM agent's template-coverage-check skill (Phase 2 WU 2.6) to cross-reference against the repo's `.specfuse/templates.yaml` declaration. Tokens are free-form kebab-case strings; matching is exact-string. Absent or empty is treated as 'no generator templates needed for this task' and trivially satisfies coverage. Populated by the human during drafting or `plan_review`; the task-decomposition skill does not infer it at v1.
